title: TA opportunity for BCOR2304
content:
Hi everyone,


My colleague, Danielle Bovenberg, asked me to share this TA opportunity with you. It might be of interest to some of you.

 

Hello! I’m Danielle Bovenberg, a new assistant professor at Leeds. I’m putting together my teaching team for the Fall B term and will be hiring 1 - 2 teaching assistants (TAs) with an affinity for strategic and entrepreneurial thinking. I’ve included some key information about the position below.

About the position

  • The course is BCOR2304, “Strategic and Entrepreneurial Thinking”. The course serves about 300 undergraduate business majors, spread across 4 sections. 

  • The course runs from October 13th to December 16th, with one meeting for the teaching team before the term kicks off in the week of October 6th. 

  • The position involves a minimum of 30 hours spread across the term.

  • The position pays $16.82 per hour. 

  • Responsibilities will include: 

    • Attending coordination meetings with me and the rest of the teaching team, weekly or bi-weekly;

    • Grading written student assignments; 

    • Providing verbal feedback to students about their assignments. 

  • I want this experience to be a valuable experience for you as a student and professional. If there is any way we can craft this position to advance your professional goals, let’s talk about it!

If you are interested

Are you interested? If so, send an email to danielle.bovenberg@colorado.edu, to set up a brief meeting in my office or on Zoom to discuss the position in greater detail.

About the position


The course is BCOR2304, “Strategic and Entrepreneurial Thinking”. The course serves about 300 undergraduate business majors, spread across 4 sections. 


The course runs from October 13th to December 16th, with one meeting for the teaching team before the term kicks off in the week of October 6th. 


The position involves a minimum of 30 hours spread across the term.


The position pays $16.82 per hour. 


Responsibilities will include: 



Attending coordination meetings with me and the rest of the teaching team, weekly or bi-weekly;

I want this experience to be a valuable experience for you as a student and professional. If there is any way we can craft this position to advance your professional goals, let’s talk about it!

author: Sina Sokhan
